steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Find angle N in radians
steps.content:
Angle M measures $\frac{\pi}{24}$ radians. Since angle N is 12 times angle M:
$$N = 12M = 12 \cdot \frac{\pi}{24} = \frac{12\pi}{24} = \frac{\pi}{2} \text{ radians}$$

steps.title: Convert π/2 radians to degrees
steps.content:
Use the conversion factor: $\text{degrees} = \text{radians} \times \frac{180}{\pi}$
$$N = \frac{\pi}{2} \times \frac{180}{\pi} = \frac{180}{2} = 90^{\circ}$$
That confirms B is correct.
